repair_candidate.summary: Repair source PR #93897 on the contributor branch so legacy plugins.entries.openai-codex data is preserved when plugins.entries.openai already exists, accurate change messages distinguish merge versus rewrite paths, true key conflicts produce warnings, and regression proof covers the dual-key migration case.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
Repair the legacy openai-codex plugin-entry migration from #93897 so dual `plugins.entries.openai` and `plugins.entries.openai-codex` configs are merged without silent data loss.

## Repair scope
- Keep the focused deep-merge behavior from source PR #93897 by @lsr911.
- Return/report accurate change messages for merge versus rewrite paths.
- Surface true key conflicts as warnings instead of silently dropping legacy values.
- Add regression coverage for the dual-key migration case.

## Credit
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911.
Issue and review context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 by @Nas01010101.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm -s vitest run src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.providers.test.ts`
- Real behavior proof workflow for #93897 after repair
validation_commands: Run the repository Real behavior proof workflow for #93897 after repair
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @lsr911 as the contributor who introduced the focused deep-merge fix in #93897.
Preserve issue and review attribution for @Nas01010101 from #93891 and the review comments on #93897.
If the executor cannot repair the contributor branch and opens a replacement PR, the replacement PR body must name #93897 as the source PR and explicitly credit @lsr911.
